title: Real Estate & Infrastructure

metrics.label: Property Registrations (Sept 2024)
metrics.context: HYDRAA demolitions cited as primary cause

metrics.label: Housing Sales Decline
metrics.context: 25% drop — largest decline among Indian cities

metrics.label: Metro Phase 2
metrics.context: Zero construction started. Rs 24,269 crore estimated cost

metrics.label: Metro Phase 1 — L&T Exit
metrics.context: Government acquiring L&T stake for Rs 15,000 crore

metrics.label: IT Exports Growth
metrics.context: Net IT jobs fell from 1,27,594 to 40,285

narrative: Property registrations in Hyderabad fell 25% year-on-year in September 2024, with HYDRAA demolitions cited as a contributing factor. Housing sales declined to 61,722 units — the largest drop among Indian cities. Metro Phase 2 (76.4 km expansion) has been approved on paper but zero construction has started. L&T is exiting Phase 1 operations after Rs 625.88 crore in cumulative losses, with the government acquiring its stake for Rs 15,000 crore. The Pharma City project has secured MoUs worth Rs 11,100 crore but no companies are operational. IT exports grew 11.2% but net new jobs fell sharply from 1,27,594 to 40,285.
